title: Parshuram ji Chalisa
intro: Shri Parshuram Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Lord Parshuram
description:
|| Shri Parashuram Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Shri Guru charan saroj chhavi, nij man mandir dhari.
Sumari Gajanana Sharada, gahi ashish Tripurari.
Buddhiheen jan janiye, avgunon ka bhandar.
Baranau Parashuram suyash, nij mati ke anusar.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Prabhu Parashuram sukh sagar, jai Munish gun gyan divakar.
Bhrigukul mukut bakat randhira, Kshatriya tej mukh sant sharira.
Jamadagni sut Renuka jaya, tej pratap sakal jag chhaya.
Maas Vaishakh sit pachchh udara, Tritiya Punarvasu manuhara.
Prahar pratham nisha sheet na ghama, tithi pradosh vyapi sukhdhama.
Tab rishi kutir rudan shishu kinha, Renuka kokhi janam hari linha.
Nij ghar uchch grah chhah thadhe, Mithun rashi Rahu sukh gadhe.
Tej-gyan mil nar tanu dhara, Jamadagni ghar Brahma avtara.
Dhara Ram shishu pavan nama, naam japat lag lah vishrama.
Bhal tripund jata sir sundar, kandhe munja janeu manhar.
Manju mekhala kati mrigchhala, Rudra mala bar vaksh vishala.
Peet basan sundar tun sohen, kandha turin dhanush man mohen.
Ved-Puran-Shruti-Smriti gyata, krodh roop tum jag vikhyata.
Dayen hath Shri Parasu uthava, Ved-Samhita bayen suhava.
Vidyavan gun gyan apara, shastra-shastra dou par adhikara.
Bhuvan charidas aru navkhanda, chauhun dishi suyash pratap prachanda.
Ek bar Ganpati ke sanga, jujhe Bhrigukul kamal patanga.
Dant tod ran kinha virama, ek danda Ganpati bhayo nama.
Kartavirya Arjuna bhupala, sahasrabahu durjan vikarala.
Suragau lakhi Jamadagni pahi, rahihahun nij ghar thani man mahi.
Mili na mangi tab kinha ladai, bhayo parajit jagat hansai.
Tan khal hriday bhayi ris gadhi, riputa muni saun atisay badhi.
Rishivar rahe dhyan lavlina, tinh par shaktighat nrip kinha.
Lagat shakti Jamadagni nipata, manahu Kshatriyakul bam vidhata.
Pitu-badh matu-rudan suni bhara, bha ati krodh man shok apara.
Kar gahi tikshan Parasu karala, dusht hanan kinheu tatkala.
Kshatriya rudhir pitu tarpan kinha, pitu-badh pratishodh sut linha.
Ikkis bar bhu Kshatriya bihini, chhin dhara bipranh kahan dini.
Jug Treta kar charit suhai, Shiv-dhanu bhang kinha Raghurai.
Guru dhanu bhanjak ripu kari jana, tab samul nash tahi thana.
Kar jori tab Ram Raghurai, vinay kinhi puni shakti dikhai.
Bhishma Drona Karna balvanta, bhaye shishya Dwapar mahan ananta.
Shastra vidya deh suyash kamava, Guru pratap digant phirava.
Charon yug tav mahima gai, sur muni manuj danuj samudai.
De Kashyap son sampada bhai, tap kinha Mahendra giri jai.
Ab laun lin samadhi natha, sakal lok navai nit matha.
Charon varn ek sam jana, samdarshi Prabhu tum Bhagvana.
Lahahin chari phal sharan tumhari, dev danuj nar bhup bhikhari.
Jo yeh padhai Shri Parasu Chalisa, tinh anukul sada Gaurisa.
Purnendu nisi basar Swami, basahun hriday Prabhu antaryami.

|| Doha ||
Parashuram ko charu charit, metat sakal agyan.
Sharan pade ko det Prabhu, sada suyash samman.

|| Shloka ||
Bhrigudev kulan bhanun, sahasrabahurmardanam.
Renuka nayan anandam, Parashum vande vipradhanam.
meaning:
|| Shri Parashuram Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Shri Guru charan saroj chhavi, nij man mandir dhari.
Holding the image of Shri Guru's lotus feet in my heart-temple.
Sumari Gajanana Sharada, gahi ashish Tripurari.
Remembering Gajanana (Ganesha) and Sharada (Saraswati), and taking the blessings of Tripurari (Shiva).
Buddhiheen jan janiye, avgunon ka bhandar.
Considering myself ignorant and a storehouse of faults.
Baranau Parashuram suyash, nij mati ke anusar.
I describe the glory of Parashuram according to my limited intellect.

|| Chaupai ||
Jai Prabhu Parashuram sukh sagar, jai Munish gun gyan divakar.
Hail Lord Parashuram, the ocean of happiness; hail the great sage, the sun of virtues and knowledge.
Bhrigukul mukut bakat randhira, Kshatriya tej mukh sant sharira.
The crown of the Bhrigu lineage, fearsome in battle, possessing the brilliance of a Kshatriya and the body of a saint.
Jamadagni sut Renuka jaya, tej pratap sakal jag chhaya.
Son of Jamadagni and Renuka, your brilliance and glory cover the entire world.
Maas Vaishakh sit pachchh udara, Tritiya Punarvasu manuhara.
Born in the month of Vaishakh, bright fortnight, on the auspicious Tritiya in Punarvasu Nakshatra.
Prahar pratham nisha sheet na ghama, tithi pradosh vyapi sukhdhama.
In the first quarter of the night, during the Pradosh tithi, you appeared as a source of happiness.
Tab rishi kutir rudan shishu kinha, Renuka kokhi janam hari linha.
The child cried in the sage's cottage; the Lord took birth from Renuka's womb.
Nij ghar uchch grah chhah thadhe, Mithun rashi Rahu sukh gadhe.
Six planets were exalted in your birth chart; Rahu in Gemini brought great happiness.
Tej-gyan mil nar tanu dhara, Jamadagni ghar Brahma avtara.
Combining brilliance and knowledge, you took a human form; the avatar of Brahma in Jamadagni's home.
Dhara Ram shishu pavan nama, naam japat lag lah vishrama.
You were given the holy name 'Ram' as a child; chanting this name brings peace.
Bhal tripund jata sir sundar, kandhe munja janeu manhar.
A tripundra on your forehead, beautiful matted hair, and a charming sacred thread on your shoulder.
Manju mekhala kati mrigchhala, Rudra mala bar vaksh vishala.
A beautiful girdle at the waist, a deer skin, and Rudra beads on your wide chest.
Peet basan sundar tun sohen, kandha turin dhanush man mohen.
Yellow garments, a beautiful quiver, and a bow on your shoulder that captivates the mind.
Ved-Puran-Shruti-Smriti gyata, krodh roop tum jag vikhyata.
Knower of Vedas, Puranas, Shrutis, and Smritis; known in the world as the form of righteous anger.
Dayen hath Shri Parasu uthava, Ved-Samhita bayen suhava.
Holding the holy Axe in the right hand, and the Vedas in the left.
Vidyavan gun gyan apara, shastra-shastra dou par adhikara.
Wise, with infinite virtues; you have authority over both scriptures and weapons.
Bhuvan charidas aru navkhanda, chauhun dishi suyash pratap prachanda.
Across the fourteen worlds and nine continents, your glory and might are fierce.
Ek bar Ganpati ke sanga, jujhe Bhrigukul kamal patanga.
Once, you clashed with Ganpati; you who are like the bee to the lotus of the Bhrigu lineage.
Dant tod ran kinha virama, ek danda Ganpati bhayo nama.
Ending the battle by breaking his tusk, Ganpati became known as Ekdanta.
Kartavirya Arjuna bhupala, sahasrabahu durjan vikarala.
King Kartavirya Arjuna, the thousand-armed, wicked and fierce.
Suragau lakhi Jamadagni pahi, rahihahun nij ghar thani man mahi.
Seeing the divine cow at Jamadagni's ashram, he decided in his mind to keep it.
Mili na mangi tab kinha ladai, bhayo parajit jagat hansai.
Not getting it by asking, he fought; he was defeated and became the laughing stock of the world.
Tan khal hriday bhayi ris gadhi, riputa muni saun atisay badhi.
His wicked heart was filled with deep anger, and his enmity with the sage grew intense.
Rishivar rahe dhyan lavlina, tinh par shaktighat nrip kinha.
The great sage was absorbed in meditation when the king attacked him.
Lagat shakti Jamadagni nipata, manahu Kshatriyakul bam vidhata.
Jamadagni fell when hit; it seemed destiny was against the Kshatriya lineage.
Pitu-badh matu-rudan suni bhara, bha ati krodh man shok apara.
Hearing of father's death and mother's cry, you were filled with intense anger and grief.
Kar gahi tikshan Parasu karala, dusht hanan kinheu tatkala.
Taking the sharp and fierce Axe in your hand, you immediately destroyed the wicked.
Kshatriya rudhir pitu tarpan kinha, pitu-badh pratishodh sut linha.
You performed the father's Tarpana with Kshatriya blood; you took revenge for your father's death.
Ikkis bar bhu Kshatriya bihini, chhin dhara bipranh kahan dini.
Twenty-one times you rid the earth of corrupt Kshatriyas and gave the land to the Brahmins.
Jug Treta kar charit suhai, Shiv-dhanu bhang kinha Raghurai.
In the Treta Yuga, the beautiful story happened when Raghurai (Ram) broke the Shiva bow.
Guru dhanu bhanjak ripu kari jana, tab samul nash tahi thana.
Seeing the breaker of the Guru's bow as an enemy, you resolved to destroy him.
Kar jori tab Ram Raghurai, vinay kinhi puni shakti dikhai.
Then Lord Ram folded his hands, prayed humbly, and showed his power.
Bhishma Drona Karna balvanta, bhaye shishya Dwapar mahan ananta.
The mighty Bhishma, Drona, and Karna were your disciples in the Dwapara Yuga.
Shastra vidya deh suyash kamava, Guru pratap digant phirava.
You earned glory by teaching the science of weapons, spreading your Guru's fame everywhere.
Charon yug tav mahima gai, sur muni manuj danuj samudai.
The four yugas sing your glory; gods, sages, humans, and demons alike.
De Kashyap son sampada bhai, tap kinha Mahendra giri jai.
Giving all wealth to Kashyap, you went to Mahendra mountain to perform penance.
Ab laun lin samadhi natha, sakal lok navai nit matha.
Even now, O Lord, you are in Samadhi; the whole world bows its head to you daily.
Charon varn ek sam jana, samdarshi Prabhu tum Bhagvana.
You see all four varnas as equal; O Lord, you are the impartial God.
Lahahin chari phal sharan tumhari, dev danuj nar bhup bhikhari.
Those who seek your refuge attain the four fruits of life.
Jo yeh padhai Shri Parasu Chalisa, tinh anukul sada Gaurisa.
Whoever reads this Shri Parashu Chalisa, Lord Shiva is always favorable to them.
Purnendu nisi basar Swami, basahun hriday Prabhu antaryami.
O Lord, who is the master of night and day, reside in my heart, O inner dweller.

|| Doha ||
Parashuram ko charu charit, metat sakal agyan.
The beautiful character of Parashuram removes all ignorance.
Sharan pade ko det Prabhu, sada suyash samman.
The Lord always gives glory and respect to those who seek his refuge.

|| Shloka ||
Bhrigudev kulan bhanun, sahasrabahurmardanam.
I salute the Parashu-wielder, the sun of the Bhrigu lineage, the destroyer of Sahasrabahu.
Renuka nayan anandam, Parashum vande vipradhanam.
The joy of Renuka's eyes, and the wealth of the Brahmins.

faq.q1: Why is the Parshuram Chalisa recited?
faq.a1: Lord Parshuram is the sixth avatar of Lord Vishnu, representing justice, righteousness, and bravery. Reciting his Chalisa instills courage, fearlessness, and the strength to fight against injustice.
faq.q2: What is the most auspicious day to recite it?
faq.a2: 'Akshaya Tritiya' (the birth anniversary of Lord Parshuram) and Tuesdays are considered the best days for his worship.
faq.a3: Absolutely not. Lord Parshuram’s anger was solely for the destruction of evil. Reciting his Chalisa reduces 'ego' and the tendency towards injustice, guiding the person on the path of righteousness (Dharma).
faq.q4: Are any special rituals required to read it?
faq.a4: No, Parshuram Ji is an ascetic avatar. You can recite it with full purity, cleanliness, and sincere devotion. There is no need for complex rituals.
faq.a5: Yes, Lord Parshuram is also a great Guru. Reciting his Chalisa helps in developing discipline, concentration, and the pursuit of knowledge.
